text.content:
As of version 0.8.0, 4e includes system-specific enrichers that will work in many text fields in the game—including powers and item descriptions, effect descriptions—as well as typed directly into chat messages. The basis of this system was lifted with gratitude from the Draw Steel system, and some of the info here is adapted from their wiki.

⚠️Beware of using copy-and-pasting the examples here! Since enrichers work in journals, we have had to insert invisible characters to preserve the source code display. If you must copy and paste the examples, be sure to delete and replace the square brackets.

Lookup

Basic Usage:[​[lookup @variableName]]

The Lookup enricher allows dynamic display of actor, item, and effect data as plain text. This works much the same as the existing use of [​[@variables]] in fields like power descriptions, but with some additional parameters and functionality. It can also be used in some areas where variables don't work, such as effect descriptions.

Lookup supports both text and numerical values, and uses roll data syntax.

Examples

  • [​[lookup @name]] Prints the actor's name.

  • [​[lookup @name]]{This character} Prints the actor's name, with a fallback to "This Character" if the lookup fails to evaluate for some reason.

  • [​[lookup @item.name]] Prints the item's name (Requires the context of an item or an effect on an item, e.g. the description)

  • [​[lookup @effect.name]] Prints the effect's name (Requires the context of an Active Effect, e.g. the description)

  • [​[lookup @name lowercase]] Prints the actor's name, but lowercase. Valid options are `capitalize`, `lowercase`, and `uppercase`.

  • [​[lookup @name style=uppercase]] The style property can be defined explicitly.

  • [​[lookup @dexMod+3 evaluate]] Evaluates the lookup value as a formula. Non-deterministic values like dice are treated as 0.

  • [​[lookup formula=@dexMod+3 evaluate=true]] The formula and evaluate parameters can be defined explicitly.

  • [​[lookup formula="@dexMod + 3" evaluate=true]] You must use quotes for formulae with spaces

Roll Prompts

This group of enrichers allow you to prompt a roll—such an an attack, check or damage roll—directly from text in an item description or chat card. You can click the link or d20 icon to make the required roll as your selected character.

Skill & Ability Checks

Basic Usage:[​[/skill ath]] / [​[/ability str]]

These enrichers create a request for a skill or ability check. The link also includes a trailing speech bubble that can be used to output a roll request to chat, with a clickable button.

Detailed Usage

  • Skills and abilities can use their abbreviations or their full names (non case-sensitive):
    [​[/skill ath]]
    [​[/ability Dexterity]]

  • Multiple skills or abilities can be passed in, separated by spaces:
    [​[/skill arc rel]]
    or identified explicitly and separated by slashes:
    [​[/ability ability=str/dex]]

  • A DC can be passed in, either as a bare number or identified explicitly:
    [​[/skill his 10]]
    [​[/ability strength dc=15]]

  • Format can be long or short (must be identified explicitly, but defaults to short if not specified):
    [​[/ability int]] produces "Intelligence"
    ​[​[/skill dun format=long]] produces "Dungeoneering check"

Attack Rolls

Basic Usage:[​[/attack formula=NumberExpression]]

Creates a link to make an attack roll using the specified formula as the attack bonus. In an item context, the attack roll will be processed with any bonuses from active effects that the power should qualify for. If targets are selected, hits will be evaluated as normal.

Detailed Usage

  • The formula can include static numbers and mathematical expressions:
    [​[/attack formula=5+3]].

  • In the context of an item's description, the item's rollData can also be used:
    [​[/attack formula=@item.attack.formula]]

  • An ability can be given with either the full name or the abbreviation. This only impacts the label and has no bearing on the roll formula.
    [​[/attack formula=10 int]]
    [​[/attack formula="@strMod" Strength]]

  • A defense can also be given, either with the full name or the abbreviation:
    [​[/attack formula="@wisMod + 2" wis fort]]
    [​[/attack formula="15" def="Armor Class"]]

  • A custom label can be provided in curly braces:
    [​[/attack formula=5]]{My Cool Attack}.

Damage/Healing

Basic Usage:[​[/damage formula=NumberExpression]] / [​[/healing formula=NumberExpression]]

Creates a link to make a damage or healing roll using the specified formula.

Detailed Usage

  • In addition to static numbers, the formula can include mathematical and dice expressions:
    [​[/attack formula=2d6]].

  • In the context of an item's description, the item's rollData can also be used:
    [​[/damage formula=@powBase]]

  • Damage can be typed in several ways. Damage types can be given explicitly as a comma separated list within quotes:
    [​[/damage formula=1d4 damageType="fire, necrotic"]]
    ...or damage types can simply be given separated by spaces:
    [​[/damage formula=1d8 cold radiant]]

  • Valid "damage" types for healing are healing and temphp * A custom label can be provided in curly braces:
    [​[/damage formula=5]]{My Cool Damage}
